summary: We recommend shipping F3 as a Desktop config snippet plus a transcript runroc rather than a literal in-Desktop screenshot because the heartbeat can prove the same MCP handshake path with three HTTP probes against the live worker and the operator can capture the in-Desktop view at a convenient operator-present tick. The tradeoff against waiting for an operator-present capture is that the worker is verifiably reachable today and the Desktop config is additive to the operator's existing two mcpServers entries pdf and MCP_DOCKER so the merge is safe to stage now. The runroc records the full initialize tools/list tools/call transcript with HTTP 200 on each call and the spec_info JSON returned in the tools/call response confirming the deterministic verifier ran in the Worker Loader sandbox.

claims.statement: docs claude-desktop-config-snippet json declares the outcomes-mcp entry via npx -y mcp-remote pointed at the deployed workers dev mcp URL with the Authorization Bearer header and the bearer token placeholder BEARER_TOKEN_HERE that the operator replaces with the value from /tmp outcomes-mcp-bearer-token-tick9 txt.

citations.title: Desktop config snippet with mcp-remote stdio shim entry
mcp-remote stdio shim that bridges Claude Desktop to a remote HTTP MCP

claims.caveats: Newer Claude Desktop builds support native remote MCP via the Custom Connectors UI which would not require mcp-remote; the snippet uses the shim to keep the path version-independent.

claims.statement: the 3-call transcript captured in F3-desktop md proves the full MCP handshake works end-to-end against the live worker because initialize returned protocolVersion 2024-11-05 with the codemode serverInfo tools/list returned exactly one tool named code with the TypeScript types for verify_outcome_pair and spec_info inlined and tools/call with JS payload return await codemode.spec_info({}) returned the spec_info JSON with canonical_floor 3.5.

citations.title: F3 desktop runroc with the 3-call transcript
outcomes-mcp source wiring codeMcpServer around the inner McpServer with verify_outcome_pair and spec_info

claims.caveats: The tools/call demonstration uses spec_info rather than verify_outcome_pair because spec_info has zero required inputs and produces an unambiguous fixed response; verify_outcome_pair takes the same path with a larger JS payload.

claims.statement: the operator's current claude_desktop_config json was observed to contain two mcpServers entries pdf and MCP_DOCKER so the snippet is additive not destructive and merging it preserves the existing servers and the preferences chromeExtension localAgentModeTrustedFolders blocks unchanged.

citations.title: F3 runroc records the observed mcpServers list before the merge
snippet install steps describe the merge-into pattern

claims.caveats: The operator's claude_desktop_config json contains preferences not just mcpServers; the snippet documents only the mcpServers addition and the operator should preserve the preferences block when merging.

open_questions: Should F3 also exercise verify_outcome_pair with a real outcome+report pair in the tools/call transcript rather than spec_info so the runroc demonstrates the full verifier path end-to-end?
methodology_notes: Listed the operator's claude_desktop_config json mcpServers block then authored the snippet against the mcp-remote stdio shim pattern. Probed the live worker with three sequential JSON-RPC calls initialize tools/list and tools/call codemode spec_info captured all three responses verbatim in the runroc.
